steps.title: Use corresponding and complementary angles
steps.content: Since triangle $RST$ is similar to triangle $UVW$, angle $R$ corresponds to angle $U$ and angle $S$ corresponds to angle $V$. In the right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary.

steps.title: Determine angle correspondence
steps.content: Since triangle RST ∼ triangle UVW with angle R ↔ angle U and angle T ↔ angle W, we have angle S ↔ angle V. Thus angle S = angle V.

steps.title: Use complementary angles to find tan(V)
steps.content:
In right triangle RST with right angle at T, angles R and S are complementary. For complementary angles, $\tan(S) = \frac{1}{\tan(R)} = \frac{1}{\frac{36}{5}} = \frac{5}{36}$. Since angle V = angle S, we have:
$$\tan(V) = \frac{5}{36} \approx 0.1389$$
